label: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 4

extraction.one_line_summary: GROVES Kaden won stage 4 sprint after MOLANO Juan Sebastián led out too early, timing his kick perfectly in the final 150m drag race.
tactical_patterns: MOLANO Juan Sebastián's UAE now contesting any terrain, suggesting broadened tactical ambition beyond pure mountain support.
GROVES Kaden won despite appearing to tire mid-sprint, revealing superior late acceleration hidden until final 100m.
Three-rider break with SEPÚLVEDA Eduardo never exceeded 90s gap, indicating peloton's calculated control to preserve sprint while allowing jersey points.
Crash outside 3km marker eliminated COQUARD Bryan and disrupted Bahrain's sprint train, exposing risk of poor positioning on roundabout-heavy finales.

rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: positive: Delivered under pressure as favorite, demonstrating mental strength and deceptive late-sprint acceleration in drag race finishes.
rider_signals.MOLANO Juan Sebastián: positive: UAE deploying him in sprint stages suggests expanded team scope beyond GC, finished second despite leading out too early.
rider_signals.SEPÚLVEDA Eduardo: neutral: Secured polka dots for EVENEPOEL Remco via controlled break, demonstrating effective domestique jersey defense tactics.
rider_signals.COQUARD Bryan: negative: Injured with suspected shoulder blade fracture after crash outside 3km, eliminating sprint option for remainder.
rider_signals.BUITRAGO Santiago: negative: Caught in same crash, raising questions about GC rider positioning awareness on technical sprint finales.
rider_signals.MENTEN Milan: positive: Third place finish indicates consistent fast-finisher capacity on uphill drag sprints.
rider_signals.VAN GESTEL Dries: positive: Fifth place shows ability to contest sprints on rising finales despite not being designated pure sprinter.
